data.title: Viper Energy hiked to Buy at Jefferies as valuation primed for upside
data.description:
Viper Energy (VNOM) up 0.8% in Monday's trading as Jefferies upgraded shares to Buy from Hold with a $55 price target, raised from $43, saying the company is well positioned to take advantage of the elevated oil price outlook and an environment that looks increasingly inflationary.

Jefferies analysts led by John Edelman said Viper (VNOM) looks cheap following a recent multiple compression to ~7.4x two-year cash flow per share tied to Diamondback Energy (FANG) share sales, and noted the company is poised to post its largest annual buyback in 2026, seeing its increased equity multiple and clean balance sheet as advantages to consolidate the Permian royalty space.

As a royalty owner with no capital commitments and relying on the discretion of operators' drilling programs, historically outperforming in inflationary service environments, Viper (VNOM) should benefit from the oil macro in both price and volume, Edelman said.

After the closing of the Warwick sale triggered 100% shareholder return payouts, the analyst modeled Viper (VNOM) leaning on the buyback in Q1 during dislocations from the early March secondary listing, adding the company was able to repurchase ~$216M worth of stock in Q1, making 2026 the largest annual buyback, and said the trajectory of elevated repurchases could continue in Q2.

data.title: IEA head says oil prices will soon reflect severity of Iran crisis
data.description:
The head of the International Energy Agency said oil prices will soon reflect how severely the Iran war has impacted shipments from the Persian Gulf. 

“Prices are already high, but they are not reflecting the severity of the problem—I agree there is a disconnect,” IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol said at an event hosted by the Atlantic Council on Monday, according to Bloomberg. “But I think soon we will see they will converge, which is an extremely sensitive issue for the global economy.”

Birol said the armed conflict has caused oil exports from that region to drop by around 13M barrels a day and damage to over 80 energy facilities. Birol added that it may take up to two years for the region to recover.

Bloomberg noted that while oil futures were currently trading at around $100 per barrel, they were below highs reached during the global financial crisis in 2008 and Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

data.title: LVMH Moët Hennessy Q1 revenue slips 6% as Middle East conflict weighs on luxury sales
data.description:
Shares of LVMH Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton (LVMHF) (LVMUY) are trading defensively on Monday as first quarter sales data showed declines in all segments on a reported basis, dragging total sales down 6% year-over-year.

On an organic basis, however, sales were up in their Wine & Spirits, Watches & Jewelry, and Selective Retailing segments, offsetting losses for Fashion & Leather Goods and flat sales in Perfumes & Cosmetics, resulting in a 1% increase in overall organic sales.

The luxury retailer reported revenue of €19.1B in Q1 maintaining “good resilience in a geopolitical and economic environment that remained disrupted, amplified by the conflict in the Middle East.”

Resilient local demand in Europe and Japan helped to offset lower tourist spending. Trends in Asia continued to improve, while the conflict in the Middle East had a negative impact of ~1% on organic growth for the quarter.

By segment, notable pockets of strength were seen in champagne sales (thanks to Formula 1 sponsorship), while cognac sales were supported by a favorable calendar effect for the Chinese new year.

LVMH’s Watches & Jewelry sales were helped by Tiffany’s “excellent performance” for the quarter thanks to the launch of a new marketing campaign, and “high jewelry” collections being showcased in Gstaad and Beijing.

In Selective Retailing, Sephora continued to achieve solid revenue growth in all regions and gain market share. During the quarter, the company’s luxury travel retailer, DFS, signed an agreement to sell its businesses in Greater China to China Tourism Group Duty Free, and its Los Angeles and San Francisco airport concessions to Duty-Free Americas.

data.title: Tesla is in the spotlight as the robotaxi rollout cuts into cash flow
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is closing in on a milestone of achieving 10B full self-driving miles traveled. Of note, Tesla's (TSLA) FSD driver-assistance system can handle most driving tasks, although it is not a fully autonomous system because drivers must still pay attention and be ready to take over. 

Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco noted that the symbolic 10B FSD miles milestone reinforces Tesla's (TSLA) autonomy lead, but with capital expenditure spending doubling and free cash flow turning negative, investors will need clearer evidence that unsupervised autonomy is around the corner to support the stock's valuation.

The bull vs. bear debate will focus on the autonomy/AI upside argument and the concerns over capex and free cash flow. Morgan Stanley currently estimates $20.7B of capex in 2026, which is relatively in line with the company's guidance of exceeding $20B, but does not yet include upside from Terafab or Tesla Solar. "At $21B of capex, we estimate an $8.4B FCF burn in 2026. If the company decides to include Terafab and Solar, we estimate its capex guidance could increase to $25-$30B this year, although there are a wide range of outcomes depending on the pace and amount of capacity that Tesla plans to add," summarized Percoco on the setup for Tesla (TSLA).

Heading into the Q1 earnings report, Tesla's (TSLA) current robotaxi activity in Austin appears to still be a small-scale operation that is moving toward less supervised driving, but it is not considered a broad commercial rollout yet. The last word on CyberCab is that initial production will begin this month.

Tesla (TSLA) is scheduled to report Q1 earnings on April 22 after the market closes. The earnings conference call will be key for investors to hear the latest on the robotaxi and CyberCab rollouts.

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) are down more than 21% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Morgan Stanley maps 60 stocks across the space trade
data.description:
Morgan Stanley is casting a wider net on the space theme.

In a note to clients, analyst Adam Jonas mapped 60 publicly traded names across the supply chain, from rare earths and industrial gases to semis, optics, launch systems and satellite communications.

The point is that the space trade is increasingly an industrial ecosystem story beyond just rocket launches and satellite operators. Morgan Stanley framed the list as a way for investors to access the theme through the companies that supply the hardware, materials, components and services that keep the sector running.

The Space 60 list is:

  • MP Materials (MP) — Rare earths

  • Almonty Industries (ALM) — Tungsten

  • Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) — Copper, molybdenum, gold, rhenium, silver

  • Alcoa (AA) — Aluminum, gallium

  • Teck Resources (TECK) — Copper, germanium, indium, cadmium

  • Carpenter Technology (CRS) — Specialty metal alloys

  • ATI (ATI) — Specialty metal alloys

  • Materion (MTRN) — Beryllium alloys, lenses, mirrors

  • Corning (GLW) — Glass, lenses, mirrors

  • Hexcel (HXL) — Composites

  • Park Aerospace (PKE) — Composites, metal assemblies

  • Linde (LIN) — Gases: LH2, LOX, LN2, CH4, He, Ar, Kr, Xe

  • Air Products (APD) — Gases: LH2, LOX, LN2, CH4, He, Ar, Kr, Xe

  • Air Liquide (AI-FR) — Gases: LH2, LOX, LN2, CH4, He, Ar, Kr, Xe

  • NewMarket (NEU) — Hydrazine, ammonium perchlorate

  • Analog Devices (ADI) — Rad-hardened semis

  • STMicro (STM) — Rad-hardened semis

  • Infineon (IFX-DE) — Rad-hardened semis

  • Microchip Technology (MCHP) — Rad-hardened semis

  • Qorvo (QRVO) — RF products, semis

  • Mercury Systems (MRCY) — RF products, semis

  • TTM Technologies (TTMI) — Printed circuit boards

  • Broadcom (AVGO) — Networking

  • Coherent (COHR) — Laser optics

  • Lumentum (LITE) — Laser optics

  • NVIDIA (NVDA) — Space-based compute

  • Teledyne (TDY) — Imaging, infrared cameras

  • RBC Bearings (RBC) — Bearings

  • Parker Hannifin (PH) — Valves

  • Ametek (AME) — Sensors, tubing, propulsion sub-components

  • Amphenol (APH) — Wire harnesses, connectors

  • Aptiv (APTV) — Wire harnesses, connectors, software

  • Moog (MOG.A) — Actuators

  • Graham Corporation (GHM) — Pumps, cryogenic equipment, thermal

  • Karman Holdings (KRMN) — Engineered propulsion components

  • Honeywell (HON) — Avionics, navigation systems

  • Redwire (RDW) — Solar arrays, structures

  • Rocket Lab (RKLB) — Launch vehicles, satellite platforms, structures

  • Boeing (BA) — Satellite platforms, launch, structures

  • Northrop Grumman (NOC) — Satellite platforms, launch

  • MDA Space (MDA) — Satellite platforms, space robotics

  • York Space Systems (YSS) — Satellite structures

  • Lockheed Martin (LMT) — Satellite structures, missiles

  • Kratos Defense (KTOS) — Ground systems

  • Firefly Aerospace (FLY) — Launch, satellite platforms, lunar landers

  • Voyager (VOYG) — Space stations, spacecraft structures

  • Intuitive Machines (LUNR) — Lunar landers

  • RTX Corp (RTX) — Satellite platforms, missiles, avionics

  • Gilat Satellite Networks (GILT) — Ground systems

  • Viasat (VSAT) — Comms satellite operator, ground systems

  • AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) — Comms satellite operator

  • SES (SESG-FR) — Comms satellite operator

  • Eutelsat Communications (ETL-FR) — Comms satellite operator

  • Iridium Communications (IRDM) — Comms satellite operator

  • Amazon (AMZN) — Comms satellite operator

  • Globalstar (GSAT) — Comms satellite operator

  • Planet Labs (PL) — Earth observation satellites

  • BlackSky Technology (BKSY) — Earth observation satellites

  • Spire Global (SPIR) — Earth observation satellites

  • Telesat (TSAT) — Comms satellite operator

data.title: Textron Aviation Defense awarded $150M contract to support military trainer aircraft
data.description:
Textron Aviation Defense, a subsidiary of Textron (TXT), has been awarded a five-year U.S. government contract valued at more than $150 million to provide engineering and program management support for the military’s T-6 trainer aircraft fleet.

The contract covers sustaining engineering and program management services for the Beechcraft T-6 Texan II aircraft used by the U.S. Air Force, U.S. Navy and U.S. Army, including the T-6A, T-6B and T-6D variants.

The award extends a prior agreement first issued in 2021, continuing support for maintenance, repairs, modifications and broader aircraft sustainment efforts. With the extension, the total potential value of the contract increases to $510 million, up from a previous ceiling of $240 million. Work under the agreement will be carried out in Wichita, Kansas.

The T-6 Texan II serves as a primary trainer aircraft for U.S. military pilots, with a fleet of more than 700 aircraft currently in service. The platform is used to prepare pilots for a range of follow-on aircraft across different branches of the armed forces.

Sustaining engineering and program management contracts typically focus on maintaining operational readiness over time, including system upgrades, lifecycle support and coordination across maintenance programs.

The award reflects ongoing demand for training infrastructure as the U.S. military continues to maintain pilot training pipelines and aircraft availability.

data.title: Crypto ETFs add $1.1B in inflows; Eric Balchunas sees 50% volume boom
data.description:
Digital asset investment products saw a strong comeback last week, with inflows hitting $1.1B. This is the highest since January. Bitcoin (BTC-USD), Ethereum (ETH-USD), XRP (XRP-USD), and others saw fresh institutional interest as risk appetite improved across the financial industry. 

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) led the market with $871M in inflows, with iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) alone contributing $612.1M. This pushed YTD BTC inflows to just under $2B. At the same time, short-bitcoin products also saw $20.2M in inflows.

This data was further strengthened by Eric Balchunas, who stated that Bitcoin (BTC-USD) ETFs “are now officially positive in YTD flows,” adding they recovered despite price pressure.

Ethereum (ETH-USD) saw $196.5M in inflows, while XRP (XRP-USD) gained $19.3M in inflows. However, Solana (SOL-USD) was the only major asset with small outflows of $2.5M.

The overall weekly ETF mood improved due to softer US spending data, CPI numbers, and easing geopolitical tension around Iran’s ceasefire, said CoinShare's Head of Research James Butterfill. 

Most of these inflows came from the US, which added $1.06B, followed by Germany with $34.6M, Canada with $7.8M, and Switzerland with $6.9M in inflows.

Eric Balchunas also noted that crypto ETFs set a quarterly record with 22T shares traded in Q1. March was the biggest volume month ever. He said ETF volume may rise to 50% higher than last year, which is a strong signal for future flows.

data.title: HSBC launches tokenized deposit service in U.S.
data.description:
HSBC Holdings (HSBC) on Monday said it is launching its tokenized deposit service in the U.S., expanding its digital money capabilities.

The company said the service combines the familiarity and trust of traditional bank deposits with the speed, transparency, and automation of blockchain-based rails.

The service lets eligible clients transfer funds 24/7.

data.title: Intel extends winning streak to nine sessions
data.description:
Shares of Intel (INTC) rose 4.71% to $65.32, in the afternoon trade on Monday, adding to a ninth session rally.

The stock has surged around 41.4% between March 31 and April 10, compared to a 4.42% rise in the S&P 500 during the same period.

The rise came amid a series of developments supporting sentiment around the semiconductor and AI space, including broader strength in chip stocks and a rebound in AI-related names following optimism around a temporary ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran.

Company-specific catalysts also contributed, with Intel announcing participation in the Terafab chip production project alongside major technology players, engaging in talks with Amazon and Google for packaging services, and benefiting from expectations of stronger server CPU demand, while continued momentum was supported by partnerships in AI and cloud infrastructure.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system, INTC is rated a Hold, with a score of 3.46 out of 5, receiving an A for profitability and momentum, but a D- in terms of valuation.

A recent Seeking Alpha analysis highlighted that Intel’s recent rally has been driven by a mix of geopolitical positioning and company-specific catalysts, including strategic buybacks, AI partnerships, and deepening ties with major technology firms.

The analyst noted that while sentiment has improved, fundamentals remain under pressure, adding that “the fundamentals are still messy, and the foundry segment is still unprofitable,” even as the stock’s strong run reflects a shift in market narrative.

While on Wall Street, nine out of 48 analysts rate the stock a buy or higher, 33 recommend a hold, and six of them suggest a sell or lower.

Shares have gained around 44% in the past month and have surged approximately 72.5% year-to-date.
